title: Making a cup of British tea_copy Elizabete
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"63bj","text":"Instructions for how to make a lovely cup of standard British 'breakfast' tea with milk (also sometimes known colloquially in the UK as builder's brew).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"19ns2","text":"Be careful when pouring boiling hot water, carrying your mug of tea, and ensure it has cooled a bit before you drink it.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fill the kettle with water and turn it on to boil. 1 L (ideally water should reach 110 °C ) Boil water in the kettle If you do not have a kettle, you can boil water in a pot on a stove (be careful not to leave it to boil dry). Or I have seen people sometimes boil water in a mug in the microwave - not my ideal approach, but it would also work.
While the kettle heats up, place 3 PG Tips tea bags in your favorite container (other brands of tea can be used, but it must be standard British black tea). 3 mg
Pour boiling water over the tea-bag in your container until about 2cm from the top of the mug
Leave the tea-bag to steep in the hot water for 00:05:00
Add some skimmed milk to your mug (about 1.5 cm). Different milk can be used according to personal preference (2%, full-fat, soy etc.)
Use spoon to squeeze the tea-bag against the side of the mug and then remove the tea-bag (dispose of tea-bag in waste bin).
Use spoon to stir your tea (I've heard people say that you should aim for your tea to be the same colour as He-man's skin - see image)
Wait for your tea to cool to a temperature that you can safely drink it at
Drink your tea (best enjoyed with some yummy shortbread or other English-style biscuits, like a Digestive as shown below ;) ) Drink and enjoy
creator: ecsamonteiro
